__fixture__.name: credential_store_locked_on_launch
__fixture__.scenario: The OS keychain is locked at Aureline launch. The credential-store lock-state record surfaces the `locked` posture with one typed retry path, an export-safe copy catalog, and the retained local capabilities that keep working without credential access. Local editing, save, undo, search, local Git, local tasks, and BYOK AI remain representable without implying credential-store availability.

copy_catalog.plain_language_summary: Aureline will ask for the keychain when you use a feature that needs it. Local editing, save, and BYOK AI keep working.

recovery_path.recovery_copy_label: Unlock the OS keychain when you next need a saved credential. Local work keeps saving to this device.

retained_local_capabilities: Open, edit, save, and undo local workspaces.
Search over the local workspace and navigate symbols.
Run local Git commands and commit locally.
Run local tasks and debuggers in trusted workspaces.
Use local model execution and BYOK AI providers that do not depend on a stored credential.
blocked_local_capabilities: Resolve stored provider tokens from the OS keychain.
Automatically sign outbound provider requests that require a keychain-backed secret.
